editorial.title: An unrepentant NEC
editorial.body: An unrepentant National Election Commission (NEC) is drawing the ire of lawmakers. While officials offered apologies, their actions suggested a lac...
editorial.pullQuote: An unrepentant National Election Commission is drawing the ire of lawmakers.
summary: NEC officials apologize but their actions contradict their words, angering lawmakers.
The National Election Commission shows no genuine remorse for its failures.
Lawmakers demand accountability beyond empty apologies from election officials.
editorial.stance: The editorial criticizes the NEC for insincere apologies lacking substantive reform.
summaryTr: NEC 관계자들이 사과했지만 그들의 행동이 말과 모순돼 국회의원들의 분노를 사고 있다.
중앙선거관리위원회는 그 실패에 대해 진정한 책임감을 보이지 않고 있다.
국회의원들은 선거 관계자들로부터 공허한 사과를 넘어선 책임 추궁을 요구하고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr:
국회의원들의 분노를 사고 있는 뻔뻔한 중앙선거관리위원회(NEC). 관계자들이 사과를 제시한 반면, 그들의 행동은 책임감 부족을 시사했다.

---

editorial.titleTr: Special Counsel in Insurrection Case Improperly Met With Presidential Chief of Staff
summaryTr: Controversy as Former Special Counsel Investigator Appointed to Presidential Chief of Staff's Civil Affairs Office
Prosecution Reform and Judicial System Overhaul Raise Questions About Special Counsel Neutrality
Personnel Appropriateness Questioned and Need for Institutional Improvements Highlighted
editorial.bodyTr: An appointment of Park Ji-young, a former special prosecutor's aide from the insurrection case, as judicial affairs secretary at the Presidential Civil Affairs Office has sparked controversy. Questions have been raised about the appropriateness of the personnel decision and the need for systemic improvements, touching on prosecutorial reform, judicial system restructuring, and the neutrality and fairness of special prosecutors.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: After Decades of 'No to Local,' Sudden Pivot to Honam for Semiconductors
summaryTr: Samsung and SK Hynix push forward with semiconductor plants worth hundreds of trillions of won in Honam region
Semiconductor industry, long centered in the capital region for decades, reviews relocation to provinces
Honam region emerges as new semiconductor hub as capital region reaches saturation
editorial.bodyTr: For decades, they have insisted that semiconductor manufacturing cannot move away from the capital region. Now Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are suddenly coordinating with the government on plans to establish semiconductor fabrication plants spanning hundreds of trillions of won in the Honam region. The stated reason is that factory sites and infrastructure in the Seoul metropolitan area are exhausted. However, for the past twenty years, the region has faced limitations in securing skilled workers and cooperation from local communities.

editorials.titleTr: Opinion: Electoral Commission Crisis Becomes Marketing Tool for 'Single-Issue Constitutional Reform
summaryTr: Moves to use the election commission crisis as justification for pushing constitutional amendments
Election commission structural reform discussed in parliamentary investigation into ballot shortage crisis
Full-time election commission chair position and audit office inspection raised as grounds for constitutional revision
editorials.bodyTr: The National Assembly's special committee investigating the ballot shortage crisis is pushing for constitutional amendments to restructure the National Election Commission and establish oversight mechanisms. Former Election Commission Chairman Roh Tae-ak and Acting Chairman Wi Chul-hwan argued that making the position of commission chairman a full-time, salaried role and subjecting the commission to audits by the Board of Audit and Inspection would be necessary steps. However, using the election commission crisis as a one-point constitutional amendment marketing strategy raises concerns about whether this represents genuine institutional reform or merely political opportunism.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: If Youth Despair 'Breaks Your Heart,' Start With Labor and Real Estate Reform
summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung acknowledged youth despair and mentioned the need for policy changes.
Young people are unable to benefit from economic growth due to a lack of stable jobs and income opportunities.
Union and real estate policy reforms are pointed out as key to solving youth issues.
editorials.bodyTr:
President Lee Jae-myung acknowledged on the 23rd that the government must painfully accept young people's sense of alienation, saying that record-breaking bonuses and stock market gains are tales from a different world. The young generation, facing a shortage of stable jobs and opportunities to build assets through income itself, represents our most pressing concern.

The government's recognition of youth despair is welcome, but it must go beyond words. What matters now is whether concrete policy changes will follow. The core problem lies not with individual effort or talent, but with structural barriers that have become entrenched in our society.

Labor union policies that protect incumbent workers at the expense of job creation for the young must be reformed. Excessive wage increases driven by seniority systems only widen the gap between regular and non-regular workers, making it harder for young people to secure stable employment. The government should push for labor market reforms that prioritize job creation and flexibility.

Real estate policy requires equally bold change. Speculative demand has inflated housing prices beyond what young people earning regular income can afford. Without fundamental reforms to tax policies and regulations that curb speculation, young people will continue to be locked out of asset formation through homeownership.

Economic growth alone cannot solve youth despair. We need structural reforms in labor and real estate policies that directly address the root causes of their alienation.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative

editorial.title: [사설] 감시 무풍지대 선관위의 끝 모를 도덕적 해이
editorial.body: 입력 2026.06.18 10:33 2026.06.18 09:43 2026.06.18 06:09 노태악 당시 중앙선거관리위원장이 지난 5일 ‘투표용지 부족 사태’ 관련 대국민 사과를 하고 있다. 뉴스1 중앙선거관리위원회가 투표용지 부족 사태를 초래한 6·3 지방선거 당시 일부 투표소에서 극심한 혼란상이 나타났던 것으로 확인되고 있다. 선거 당일 상황을 기록한 투표록에 따르면, 서울 잠실2동 한 투표소에선 투표관리관 도장이 찍히지 않은 채 용지가 배부됐다. 다른 투표소에선 바닥에 투표용지가 떨어져 있거나, 용지 부족 때문에 선관위에 전화를 걸어도 연락이 닿지 않았다고 한다. 추가 투표용지가 도착한 뒤에도 대기표를 받았던 주민들이 돌아오지 않아 참정권 행사가 불발됐다. 지난해 대선 때도 다른 사람의 선거인명부에 서명하고 투표한 사례가 2000건이 넘는 등 선거 관리 전반의 부실이 심각하다. 다 이유가 있었다. 선거로 업무가 바빠지는 해에 휴직자가 오히려 늘어나는 게 이번뿐 아니라 선관위의 전통이라고 한다. 이 조직이 공정한 선거 관리를 맡을 자격이 있는지 의문을 갖게 한다. 문제는 부실에서 그치지 않는다. 선관위 지도부부터 심각한 도덕적 해이에 빠져 있었다는 사실이 속속 드러나고 있다. 노태악 전 중앙선관위원장은 지난해 덴마크·스웨덴을 비롯해 재임 중 세 차례 간 해외 출장에 모두 배우자를 동반했다. 1200만원이 넘는 비즈니스 항공권 등의 경비를 선관위가 지불했다. 공식적인 외교나 국가 중대사도 아닌 해외 선거 관련 방문에 세금을 흥청망청 쓴 것이어서 외유성 출장이라는 비판을 면키 어렵다. 외부에 공개되는 출장 보고서에 ‘부부 동반’ 문구를 넣지 않은 것만 봐도 명백한 특혜가 아닐 수 없다. 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다. 선관위 직원들은 몰디브 등 세계적인 휴양지로 출장을 가고 선거 홍보물로 활용된다며 해변 사진을 버젓이 보고서에 첨부했다. 역량 강화를 이유로 이탈리아 등 관광지에도 매년 직원을 보냈다. 심지어 선관위 고위직들의 ‘자녀 특혜 채용’ 의혹이 불거진 시기에도 외유성 출장이 계속될 정도로 내부 기강 해이가 심각하다. 독립성을 방패 삼아 외부의 감시와 견제를 피해 성역을 구축해 온 선관위는 전면적인 개혁 없이는 국민의 신뢰를 회복할 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다. 예산 집행의 투명성 확보와 함께 채용 비리를 차단할 감시 시스템이 도입돼야 한다. 신뢰를 잃은 선관위가 관리하는 선거는 결과에 대한 불신의 씨앗을 남길 우려가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다.
summary: 6·3 지방선거에서 투표용지 부족으로 극심한 혼란 발생, 선관위 관리 부실 심각
노태악 전 위원장이 배우자 동반 해외출장에 1200만원대 세금 낭비
선관위는 독립성을 방패 삼아 감시를 피해온 후 전면 개혁 없이는 신뢰 회복 불가
editorial.stance: 선관위의 독립성 뒤에 숨은 적폐를 척결하고 예산·채용 감시 시스템을 도입해 민주주의의 기초인 선거 신뢰를 회복해야 한다
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: The Election Commission's Bottomless Moral Hazard in the Surveillance Gray Zone
summaryTr: Severe chaos erupted during the June 3 local elections due to ballot shortages, revealing serious negligence in election commission management
Former Chairman Noh Tae-ak wasted over 12 million won in taxpayer money on overseas business trips accompanied by his spouse
The election commission has evaded oversight by using independence as a shield, and trust cannot be restored without comprehensive reform
editorial.bodyTr: The National Election Commission has confirmed that severe chaos occurred at some polling places during the June 3 local elections that led to the ballot shortage crisis. According to voting records documenting the situation on election day, at one polling station in Jamsil 2-dong, Seoul, ballots were distributed without the election supervisor's stamp. At other polling stations, ballots were found scattered on the floor, and residents reported that calls to the election commission went unanswered due to ballot shortages. Even after additional ballots arrived, residents who had received waiting tickets did not return, resulting in lost voting opportunities. During last year's presidential election, there were over 2,000 cases of people signing other voters' names and casting ballots, indicating serious mismanagement across the entire election administration system. There were reasons for this. According to reports, it is a tradition at the election commission that the number of employees on leave actually increases during years when election work becomes busy. This raises questions about whether this organization is qualified to manage fair elections. The problem extends beyond mere negligence. It is becoming increasingly clear that the election commission's leadership has fallen into serious ethical lapses. Former Election Commission Chairman Roh Tae-ak accompanied his spouse on all three overseas business trips during his tenure, including visits to Denmark and Sweden. The election commission paid for business-class airfare exceeding 12 million won and other expenses. Using taxpayer money for overseas visits related to elections that were neither official diplomatic missions nor major state affairs is difficult to defend against criticism of junket travel. The fact that official trip reports did not mention "accompanied by spouse" makes clear that this was outright favoritism. Given the conduct of the agency head, employee misconduct was virtually inevitable. Election commission staff went on business trips to world-renowned vacation destinations like the Maldives and brazenly attached beach photos to reports, claiming they would be used in election promotional materials. They sent employees yearly to tourist destinations like Italy under the guise of capacity building. Internal discipline has deteriorated so severely that even when suspicions arose of "nepotistic hiring" among senior election commission officials, junket travel continued unabated. The election commission, using independence as a shield to avoid external oversight and checks while creating a sanctuary, has reached a point where it cannot recover public trust without comprehensive reform. Transparency in budget execution must be secured, and a monitoring system to prevent hiring irregularities must be introduced. Elections managed by an election commission that has lost public trust risk leaving seeds of doubt about their outcomes.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 공공기관의 도덕적 해이와 특권, 투명성 부족을 강하게 비판하며 감시 체계 강화를 요구하는 진보적 프레이밍으로, 권력 기관의 부실과 기강 해이에 대한 진보진영의 전형적 관점을 드러냄.

editorial.title: A different yardstick
editorial.body: President Lee's defense of startup initiative clashes with his past rhetoric on data breaches The recent leak of ideas submitted by aspiring entrepreneurs applying for a government startup-support program has sent shockwaves through the startup community. The Ministry of SMEs and Startups' "Startup for All" project is an online platform that hosts a nationwide startup competition. The winner is eligible for up to 1 billion won ($651,000) in financial support, including prize money. According to the ministry, the email addresses, summaries of business ideas, and judges' evaluations of 5,000 first-round qualifiers were leaked. While detailed business plans were not among the leaked materials, the incident could give rise to far-reaching repercussions that may be difficult to contain, including technology theft. Even more shocking is the fact that the entity identified as being responsible for the leak is an AI company selected by the government as an official partner to help young people launch startups. The incident exposes how poorly the project was managed. The ministry announced damage control measures, including free registration for trade-secret original certification, technology escrow services and legal advice. However, while trade-secret original certification can be used as evidence that a trade secret existed and who first possessed it in the event of a dispute, it does not confer exclusive rights like a patent. In other words, even if someone starts a business based on an idea similar to one that was leaked, trade-secret original certification does not give the holder the legal right to prevent it. Technology escrow protects technology by depositing it with a government-designated institution, but only companies with a business registration certificate are eligible to use it. It is questionable how helpful the measures will be. The "Startup for All" initiative was rolled out in earnest after President Lee Jae Myung said at a startup strategy meeting on Jan. 30 that support was needed to help people take their first steps before starting a business. In only about four months, it has expanded into a major project drawing more than 60,000 applicants. The government needs to reflect on whether, in its eagerness to spark a startup boom, it pushed ahead too hastily with the program. President Lee has said that companies should face consequences severe enough to make them think they could collapse if they violate information security regulations and harm the public. He has also directed the introduction of punitive fines. The CEOs of both Coupang and Lotte Card resigned to take responsibility for the leak of customers' personal data. Coupang was hit with a record 624.7 billion won penalty. Han Seong-sook, the prime minister nominee who also serves as Minister of SMEs and Startups, apologized Monday for the incident. Despite criticism over the poor management of the "Startup for All" project and questions about the effectiveness of the measures to deal with the damage, Lee endorsed the program at a Cabinet meeting Tuesday, stating that "the scale, intensity and frequency of support should be expanded." Lee also praised Han, saying that the program had drawn more participants than expected, generated strong enthusiasm, and produced many promising business ideas. These remarks stand in stark contrast to his previous comments on data leaks at private companies. Han's confirmation hearing begins today. Because of resistance from the ruling party, not a single witness has been called. It is hard to expect a thorough vetting. If she is allowed to escape accountability with nothing more than an apology on her way to work, clears the confirmation hearing, and is appointed prime minister, not many people would consider that fair in comparison with the private companies that were held accountable. Entrepreneurship involves people's livelihoods and future. Instead of forging ahead with a second open call for applications, the government should first take a careful look at how these problems came about. Above all, it can hardly regain the public trust if it does not take responsibility when things go wrong. summary: A data breach at South Korea's government startup program exposed 5,000 applicants' ideas and contact info.
President Lee endorsed expanding the program despite the leak, contradicting his tough stance on corporate data breaches.
Critics question whether the PM nominee will face real accountability or escape with just an apology.
editorial.stance: Government hypocrisy: holding private firms to strict data security standards while shielding its own mismanaged projects from consequences.
summaryTr: 한국 정부 스타트업 프로그램의 개인정보 유출 사고로 지원자 5,000명의 아이디어와 연락처가 노출됐다.
이 대통령은 유출 사건에도 불구하고 프로그램 확대를 지지했으며, 이는 기업 개인정보 유출에 대한 강경한 입장과 모순된다.
비판론자들은 총리 후보자가 실질적 책임을 질 것인지, 아니면 사과만으로 빠져나갈 것인지 의문을 제기하고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr:
이준석 대통령의 스타트업 지원 사업 옹호, 과거 데이터 유출 발언과 충돌

정부의 스타트업 지원 프로그램에 지원한 예비 창업자들이 제출한 아이디어가 유출된 사건은 스타트업 커뮤니티에 큰 충격을 안겼다. 중소벤처기업부의 '모두의 스타트업' 사업은 전국 스타트업 대회를 개최하는 온라인 플랫폼이다. 우승자는 상금을 포함해 최대 10억 원의 재정 지원을 받을 수 있다. 정부에 따르면 1차 선정 5천 명의 이메일 주소, 사업 아이디어 요약, 심사위원 평가가 유출됐다. 상세한 사업 계획서는 유출된 자료에 포함되지 않았지만, 이번 사건은 기술 탈취 등 통제하기 어려운 광범위한 후속 문제를 초래할 수 있다. 더욱 충격적인 것은 유출 책임이 있는 것으로 파악된 주체가 정부가 청년 창업 지원을 위해 공식 파트너로 선정한 AI 회사라는 점이다. 이번 사건은 이 사업이 얼마나 부실하게 관리됐는지를 드러낸다. 정부는 영업비밀 보호 인증 무료 등록, 기술 에스크로 서비스, 법률 자문 등 피해 수습 대책을 발표했다. 그러나 영업비밀 보호 인증은 분쟁 시 영업비밀이 존재했고 누가 처음 소유했는지를 입증하는 증거로만 사용될 수 있으며, 특허처럼 독점적 권리를 부여하지 않는다. 즉, 유출된 아이디어와 유사한 아이디어로 창업하더라도 영업비밀 보호 인증 소유자는 이를 법적으로 저지할 수 없다. 기술 에스크로는 기술을 정부 지정 기관에 위탁하여 보호하지만, 사업자등록증을 보유한 회사만 이용할 수 있다. 이런 대책들이 얼마나 도움이 될지는 의문의 여지가 있다. '모두의 스타트업' 사업은 이준석 대통령이 1월 30일 스타트업 전략회의에서 창업 전 첫발을 내딛도록 지원이 필요하다고 언급한 후 본격적으로 추진되었다. 불과 4개월 만에 6만여 명이 넘는 지원자를 모은 대규모 사업으로 확대됐다. 정부는 스타트업 붐을 일으키려는 성급함 속에서 이 사업을 너무 성급하게 추진하지 않았는지 되돌아봐야 한다. 이준석 대통령은 과거 정보 보안 규정을 어기고 국민에게 피해를 끼친 기업들이 폐업까지 고려할 정도의 심각한 책임을 져야 한다고 말했다. 그는 또한 과징금 도입을 지시했다. 쿠팡과 롯데카드의 CEO는 개인정보 유출 책임을 지고 사임했다. 쿠팡은 기록적인 6247억 원의 과징금을 부과받았다. 중소벤treated기업부 장관을 겸임하는 총리 후보자 한성숙은 월요일 이번 사건에 대해 사과했다. '모두의 스타트업' 사업의 부실 관리와 피해 대책의 실효성에 대한 비판에도 불구하고, 이준석 대통령은 화요일 국무회의에서 "지원의 규모, 강도, 빈도를 확대해야 한다"고 이 사업을 옹호했다. 또한 이 대통령은 이 사업이 예상보다 많은 참여자를 모았고, 높은 열정을 불러일으켰으며, 유망한 사업 아이디어를 많이 배출했다며 한 장관을 칭찬했다. 이러한 발언은 민간 기업의 데이터 유출에 대한 그의 과거 발언과는 완전히 대조적이다. 한 장관의 인준청문회가 오늘 시작된다. 여당의 저항으로 증인 소환이 단 한 건도 이루어지지 않았다. 철저한 검증을 기대하기 어렵다. 만약 그녀가 출근길 사과만으로 책임을 피하고 인준청문회를 통과하여 총리로 임명된다면, 책임을 진 민간 기업들과 비교할 때 공정하다고 생각할 사람은 많지 않을 것이다. 창업은 국민의 생계와 미래와 관련된 문제다. 정부는 두 번째 공모 신청을 진행하기보다는 먼저 이런 문제들이 어떻게 발생했는지 꼼꼼히 살펴봐야 한다. 무엇보다 문제가 발생했을 때 책임지지 않는다면 국민의 신뢰를 되찾기는 어려울 것이다.

editorials.title: A different yardstick
editorials.body: President Lee's defense of startup initiative clashes with his past rhetoric on data breaches The recent leak of ideas submitted by aspiring entrepreneurs applying for a government startup-support program has sent shockwaves through the startup community. The Ministry of SMEs and Startups' "Startup for All" project is an online platform that hosts a nationwide startup competition. The winner is eligible for up to 1 billion won ($651,000) in financial support, including prize money. According to the ministry, the email addresses, summaries of business ideas, and judges' evaluations of 5,000 first-round qualifiers were leaked. While detailed business plans were not among the leaked materials, the incident could give rise to far-reaching repercussions that may be difficult to contain, including technology theft. Even more shocking is the fact that the entity identified as being responsible for the leak is an AI company selected by the government as an official partner to help young people launch startups. The incident exposes how poorly the project was managed. The ministry announced damage control measures, including free registration for trade-secret original certification, technology escrow services and legal advice. However, while trade-secret original certification can be used as evidence that a trade secret existed and who first possessed it in the event of a dispute, it does not confer exclusive rights like a patent. In other words, even if someone starts a business based on an idea similar to one that was leaked, trade-secret original certification does not give the holder the legal right to prevent it. Technology escrow protects technology by depositing it with a government-designated institution, but only companies with a business registration certificate are eligible to use it. It is questionable how helpful the measures will be. The "Startup for All" initiative was rolled out in earnest after President Lee Jae Myung said at a startup strategy meeting on Jan. 30 that support was needed to help people take their first steps before starting a business. In only about four months, it has expanded into a major project drawing more than 60,000 applicants. The government needs to reflect on whether, in its eagerness to spark a startup boom, it pushed ahead too hastily with the program. President Lee has said that companies should face consequences severe enough to make them think they could collapse if they violate information security regulations and harm the public. He has also directed the introduction of punitive fines. The CEOs of both Coupang and Lotte Card resigned to take responsibility for the leak of customers' personal data. Coupang was hit with a record 624.7 billion won penalty. Han Seong-sook, the prime minister nominee who also serves as Minister of SMEs and Startups, apologized Monday for the incident. Despite criticism over the poor management of the "Startup for All" project and questions about the effectiveness of the measures to deal with the damage, Lee endorsed the program at a Cabinet meeting Tuesday, stating that "the scale, intensity and frequency of support should be expanded." Lee also praised Han, saying that the program had drawn more participants than expected, generated strong enthusiasm, and produced many promising business ideas. These remarks stand in stark contrast to his previous comments on data leaks at private companies. Han's confirmation hearing begins today. Because of resistance from the ruling party, not a single witness has been called. It is hard to expect a thorough vetting. If she is allowed to escape accountability with nothing more than an apology on her way to work, clears the confirmation hearing, and is appointed prime minister, not many people would consider that fair in comparison with the private companies that were held accountable. Entrepreneurship involves people's livelihoods and future. Instead of forging ahead with a second open call for applications, the government should first take a careful look at how these problems came about. Above all, it can hardly regain the public trust if it does not take responsibility when things go wrong. editorials.pullQuote: President Lee has said companies should face consequences severe enough to make them think they could collapse.
summary: A data breach at South Korea's government startup program exposed 5,000 applicants' ideas and contact info.
President Lee endorsed expanding the program despite the leak, contradicting his tough stance on corporate data breaches.
Critics question whether the PM nominee will face real accountability or escape with just an apology.
editorials.stance: Government hypocrisy: holding private firms to strict data security standards while shielding its own mismanaged projects from consequences.
summaryTr: 한국 정부 스타트업 프로그램의 개인정보 유출 사고로 지원자 5,000명의 아이디어와 연락처가 노출됐다.
이 대통령은 유출 사건에도 불구하고 프로그램 확대를 지지했으며, 이는 기업 개인정보 유출에 대한 강경한 입장과 모순된다.
비판론자들은 총리 후보자가 실질적 책임을 질 것인지, 아니면 사과만으로 빠져나갈 것인지 의문을 제기하고 있다.

editorial.title: [사설] 막 오른 민주당 전당대회, 비전과 정책으로 승부해야
editorial.body: 정청래 더불어민주당 대표가 24일 최고위원회의에서 대표직에서 사퇴하며 “오늘 당대표직을 내려놓지만 이재명 정부의 성공을 위해서 제가 서 있는 위치에서 최선을 다하겠다”고 밝혔다. 6·3 지방선거 이후 비당권파들로부터 불출마 압박을 받아왔지만, 8·17 전당대회에서 대표
editorial.pullQuote: 당대표직을 내려놓지만 이재명 정부의 성공을 위해 최선을 다하겠다
summary: 정청래 민주당 대표가 전당대회를 앞두고 대표직 사퇴를 선언했다.
지방선거 패배 후 비당권파의 불출마 압박을 받아온 상황이다.
민주당은 비전과 정책으로 새 지도부 선출에 임해야 한다.
editorial.stance: 민주당은 내분 극복하고 비전 있는 지도자 선출로 재도약해야 한다
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: With Democrats' Convention Underway, Party Must Compete on Vision and Policy
summaryTr: Democratic Party leader Jung Chul-rae announced his resignation as party chief ahead of the national convention.
He has faced pressure from the anti-establishment faction not to run following the party's defeat in local elections.
The Democratic Party must select new leadership based on vision and policy.
editorial.bodyTr: Jung Chul-rae, the Democratic Party leader, resigned from his position as party chief at the Supreme Council meeting on the 24th, stating, "While I am stepping down from the party leadership today, I will do my best in my current position for the success of the Lee Jae-myung administration." After the June 3 local elections, he had faced pressure from non-party faction members to not run for re-election, but at the August 17 party convention, he was elected as party leader.

leanScore.label: Center

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: With Democrats' Convention Underway, Party Must Compete on Vision and Policy
summaryTr: Democratic Party leader Jung Chul-rae announced his resignation as party chief ahead of the national convention.
He has faced pressure from the anti-establishment faction not to run following the party's defeat in local elections.
The Democratic Party must select new leadership based on vision and policy.
editorials.bodyTr: Jung Chul-rae, the Democratic Party leader, resigned from his position as party chief at the Supreme Council meeting on the 24th, stating, "While I am stepping down from the party leadership today, I will do my best in my current position for the success of the Lee Jae-myung administration." After the June 3 local elections, he had faced pressure from non-party faction members to not run for re-election, but at the August 17 party convention, he was elected as party leader.

editorials.bodyTr: The United Nations Command said on the 24th that North Korea's installation of barbed wire fences and other work being conducted north of the Demilitarized Zone does not constitute a violation of the armistice agreement. With the UN Command releasing explanatory materials and appearing to publicly counter the Ministry of Defense, tensions between South Korea and the United States over 'jurisdiction of the DMZ' have become apparent.

The appointment of a politically controversial figure to a public institution signals coming debate.

editorial.titleTr: Semiconductor Cluster in Honam Region Hinges on Power and Water Infrastructure
summaryTr: The government is pushing to establish a semiconductor cluster in Honam.
Honam was selected due to power and water shortages in the Seoul metropolitan area.
The sufficiency of Honam's infrastructure should be reviewed first.
editorial.bodyTr: Presidential Chief Policy Officer Kim Yong-beom said on the 24th that "discussions on establishing a semiconductor cluster in Honam (Gwangju and South Jeolla Province) are in the final stages." Regarding the decision to locate it in Honam, far from the Yongin semiconductor cluster currently under construction, he cited the reason that "the capital region lacks electricity and water resources." editorials.stance: 반도체 산업 기지화를 위해 기초 인프라 확충이 필수적이라는 입장
editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Building Semiconductor Hub in Honam Region...The Challenge Lies in Securing Power, Water and Workforce
summaryTr: A semiconductor production base is being developed in the Honam region.
Securing electricity, water, and workforce are critical factors for success.
Infrastructure development to revitalize the regional economy is urgently needed.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: 산업 기지화와 인프라 구축을 지지하는 보수적 경제 프레임과 지역 경제 활성화를 강조하는 진보적 관심이 중립적으로 결합되어 있으며, 좌우 이념적 비판이나 특정 정치 진영에 대한 노골적 옹호가 없음.
